framework.name: LangGraph
framework.description: State graph execution engine inspired by Google's Pregel — super-steps with message-passing between computation nodes. Built by LangChain.

modules.name: State (StateGraph)
modules.description: Shared data structure (TypedDict/Pydantic) defining the graph's truth source. Reducer functions merge partial updates from each node.

modules.name: Nodes
modules.description: Computation units — Python functions or LangChain runnables that receive State and return partial updates. Includes ToolNode for tool execution.

modules.name: Edges
modules.description: Control flow: normal edges (fixed A→B), conditional edges (routing function inspects State), entry points.

modules.name: Checkpointer
modules.description: Persists state after every super-step per thread_id. Enables recovery, time-travel debugging, and human-in-the-loop.

modules.name: Store (BaseStore)
modules.description: Cross-thread long-term memory — key-value namespace store shared across conversation threads.

modules.name: Command
modules.description: Unified primitive combining state update + routing in one return value: Command(update=..., goto=...).

modules.name: Pregel Runner
modules.description: Execution loop — activates nodes by super-step, collects messages, applies reducers, votes to halt when all idle.

metadata.description: Pregel Runner activates nodes that received messages on incoming channels
Each node reads the current State at the start of its invocation
Nodes return partial state updates merged via reducers
After execution, the node's exit triggers edge traversal
Edges determine which nodes receive messages in the next super-step
State snapshot saved after each super-step
Recover state from any checkpoint for replay or human intervention
Command(update=...) merges state and Command(goto=...) routes simultaneously
Nodes access long-term memory across conversation threads

phases.name: Super-Step Execution
phases.description: Pregel super-step cycle: activate nodes → execute → update state → checkpoint → route to next

steps.description: Runner activates all nodes that received messages in their incoming channels
Each activated node reads current State via state-read protocol
Node function runs with State as input, produces partial update
Partial update merged into State using channel-specific reducer functions
State snapshot persisted per thread_id for recovery
Exit edges determine destination nodes; conditional edges inspect State for branching

phases.name: Halt Vote & Termination
phases.description: All nodes vote on whether to continue; execution ends when all are idle and no messages in transit

steps.description: Each node votes. Idle nodes vote to halt. If all idle and no messages in transit → terminate

phases.name: Human-in-the-Loop Interrupt
phases.description: Optional: execution pauses at marked nodes for human approval, then resumes via Command

steps.description: Human provides approval/edit via Command(update, goto), execution resumes
